title: Lock v0.3.0 post-release distribution state
reason: Lock the factual post-release distribution state after v0.3.0 release asset verification, README install status update, curl installer verification, and Homebrew tap planning, without claiming unavailable package-manager support or adding runtime behavior.
proposed_changes: Record that v0.3.0 release binaries are Available only for verified GitHub Release archives and checksums.
Record that the curl installer is Available only for verified v0.3.0 release assets after install.sh verification.

Keep Homebrew Planned until a tap/formula is published and tested, keep no-terminal mode assisted rather than deterministic validation without CLI proof, keep model workspace packs as UX, and preserve ni-kernel as pre-runtime.
risk_impact: Strengthens the existing high-severity packaging-claim risk by replacing older release-gated wording with evidence-bound availability for release binaries and curl while preserving planned Homebrew status and pre-runtime boundaries.
readiness_impact: Expected to remain READY because accepted CAP-017 keeps requirements, evaluations, mitigated high risks, artifacts, non-goals, and no blocker questions while aligning the contract with verified v0.3.0 distribution evidence.
